headline: Wall Street's Earnings Expectations Are Sky High
summary: Investor expectations are high heading into the earnings season, leaving lots of room for disappointment.  Consensus estimates signal S&P 500 earnings to grow 22% from the year-ago period, that’s after more than 27% growth in the first quarter, Bank of America reported.  “Expectations are elevated, but we see no signs of earnings momentum rolling over,” Bank of America strategists said, issuing an earnings forecast that sat above consensus estimates and lifting their full-year EPS outlook.

headline: Shares of several energy companies are trading higher as stocks rebound following Monday's bank-driven weakness. Additionally, February CPI data showed inflation in line with expectations. Also, due to increased financial stability concerns, investors are expecting a softer rate hike, which could benefit the sector.

headline: Big bank earnings on tap: Why these 2 could surprise to the upside
summary: Big banks are kicking off second quarter earnings season on Tuesday, Jul. 14, with reports from JPMorgan (JPM), Goldman Sachs (GS), Wells Fargo (WFC), Bank of America (BAC), and Citigroup (C). Morgan Stanley (MS) posts its results on Wednesday. RBC Capital Markets analyst Gerard Cassidy shares which banks he thinks could surprise markets and investors.
